pivot_reason: CAT as main contribution was too thin — our CAT-guided outputs (relational meaning, alternative appraisals) are essentially the same as HVGuard's implicit meaning and RAMF's adversarial reasoning. GPT integrity test confirmed: removing 'CAT' from paper wouldn't change the method.
new_direction: Perception-to-Cognition (P2C) paradigm, inspired by Vad-R1 (NeurIPS 2025). Reframes hate detection as a staged cognitive process rather than describe-then-judge.

v13_pipeline.LLM: gpt-5.4-nano, single call per video, P2C-CoT structured reasoning
v13_pipeline.Output_fields: 6 tagged fields (which, what, target, where, why, how), unified across hateful/non-hateful
v13_pipeline.Embeddings: perception (step1+step2 BERT), cognition (step3+step4 BERT), answer (full answer BERT)

next_steps: 1. Wait for v13 LLM generation to complete on HateMM
3. Run main_v13.py to train fusion + evaluate
5. If v13 ≥ v12: run on EN MHC and ZH MHC
6. If v13 < v12: iterate on prompt design
7. Run controlled baselines (Generic CoT, RAMF-style, etc.)
8. Update paper with new paradigm framing
